summary: runtimedev-link ships a persistent C2 agent with remote command execution, filesystem exfiltration, and cross-platform autostart
details: The package presents itself as 'Pure Node.js telemetry for RuntimeDev platform' but implements a full remote-access backdoor. lib/transport.js polls POST /api/telemetry/poll-command against an operator-supplied apiBase (SSTAR_API_BASE / NODE_LINK_API_BASE) and dispatches server-returned actions: (1) `command` — arbitrary shell commands are executed via child_process.execSync and results POSTed to /api/telemetry/command-result; (2) `directoryScan` — walks the user's home directory (up to depth 50, 500 entries via fs_scan.js) and POSTs the tree to /api/telemetry/directory-scan-result; (3) `downloadRequest` — zips any path on disk and uploads the base64 contents to /api/telemetry/upload-download. postTelemetryReport periodically exfiltrates hostname, username, OS info, public IP (fetched from api.ipify.org), and a full inventory of installed Chrome/Edge/Brave extensions read from OS-specific User Data profile directories. persistence.js installs hidden, reboot-persistent autostart across all platforms: Linux systemd user unit (Restart=always) with `@reboot` cron fallback, macOS LaunchAgent plist (RunAtLoad + KeepAlive), and Windows Task Scheduler XML with LogonTrigger and Hidden=true launched via wscript.exe /B. portable_runtime.js downloads a full Node.js 22.22.0 tarball from nodejs.org into ~/.local/share/runtimedev-link/runtime, chmods it +x, and the persistence script uses that runtime to run the agent — decoupling the backdoor from the installer's system Node. The 'telemetry' naming (endpoints /api/telemetry/*, function names like postTelemetryReport) is a cover story for a fully-functional remote-access agent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
